mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations by raising discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, generating negative forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-increase periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, defined as the unemployment rate rising more than 0.5 percentage points over three months, signals an impending recession that reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where the threshold is crossed, or the signal fails to predict direction during actual recession onsets.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling housing starts indicates consumer substitution from durable goods to services while the housing sector collapses, creating a fragile demand environment that triggers negative equity returns within one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no statistical edge (Rank IC < 0.02) or housing starts decline without corresponding retail sales increases during negative return periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports short-term equity momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week, but only during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during mid-cycle expansions, producing negative returns in >50% of positive growth instances, or loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth coincides with negative equity returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation measured by the consumer price index exceeds 4.0%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of periods where inflation crosses the 4.0% threshold, or the signal fails to predict downturns during stagflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of synchronized expansion periods, or the correlation between GDP growth and retail sales breaks down during financial stress.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production declines while employment remains stable indicates falling productivity and rising unit labor costs, which erodes profit margins and triggers negative equity returns within one month during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle expansions, or positive returns occur in >55% of instances where the divergence condition is met.
